//1: Project-level enforcement of CLAUDE.md MCP-first workflow.
//3: PreToolUse hooks fire BEFORE Edit/Write/MultiEdit on .svelte / .rs files.
//4: Each hook surfaces the MCP-first rule via stderr and exits 1 (non-blocking
//5: warning). The model sees the stderr in its next turn as a system reminder
//6: and self-enforces. Exit 2 would block the tool call entirely; we don't do
//7: that because the hook is stateless and can't tell whether the model already
//8: invoked the MCP earlier in the session — blocking would livelock retries.

hooks.command: jq -er '.tool_input.file_path // empty' | grep -qE '\.svelte$' && { echo '⚠️  CLAUDE.md MCP-FIRST — editing a .svelte file. BEFORE writing code, you MUST: (1) call mcp__svelte__list-sections; (2) call mcp__svelte__get-documentation for every section whose use_cases matches your change (runes, lifecycle, snippets, $effect, $derived, etc.); (3) THEN write code; (4) call mcp__svelte__svelte-autofixer until clean. The autofixer is the LAST gate, not the only one. In your next response cite which docs sections you pulled before editing.' >&2; exit 1; } || exit 0

jq -er '.tool_input.file_path // empty' | grep -qE '\.rs$' && { echo '⚠️  CLAUDE.md MCP-FIRST — editing a .rs file. Use rust-analyzer MCP: (1) mcp__rust-analyzer__rust_analyzer_set_workspace pinned to backend/ (once per session); (2) mcp__rust-analyzer__rust_analyzer_workspace_diagnostics instead of cargo check; (3) mcp__rust-analyzer__rust_analyzer_format on every edited file before commit; (4) cargo clippy -D warnings as the FINAL gate. Fall back to cargo CLI ONLY if the MCP server is unresponsive.' >&2; exit 1; } || exit 0
